description: Company Information Infrastructure Upgrade — Normalize company data into 6 purpose-built Postgres tables (companies, company_financials, company_fundamentals + history SCD-2, company_competitors, institutional_holders, insider_transactions). Add Redis (ElastiCache) cache-aside via BaseRepository pattern. Aggregate via CompanyService.get_full_profile() with ThreadPoolExecutor parallel fan-out and on-demand backfill guarded by per-(ticker, domain) Redis mutex. Expose unified GET /api/company/<ticker>/profile endpoint. Refactor existing /api/market/<symbol>/* routes to use new repos (backward compatible). Backfill from existing company_data_cache JSON blobs, then deprecate. Add scheduled EDGAR (10-Q/K, 13F, Form 4) and Polygon fundamentals ingestion via APScheduler with window-aware 13F polling and monthly SEC calendar probe. Seed S&P 500 core tickers up front (rate-gated) with tighter refresh cadence; long-tail tickers grow organically via on-demand backfill. Stay synchronous Flask + SQLAlchemy 2.0 with clean repo interfaces designed for a future mechanical async migration. Source PRD: tasks/prd-company-info-infra.md

userStories.implementation_notes:
Added Redis to dev compose + prod CDK and wired a health endpoint.

Files touched:
- docker-compose.yml: new `redis` service using `redis:7-alpine` with `--appendonly yes`, named volume `redis-data`, and redis-cli PING healthcheck. Added `REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379/0` env var to `signal` and `feed-worker`, plus `depends_on.redis.service_healthy` so the app waits for Redis.
- docker-compose.override.yml: publishes Redis on host `6379:6379` for dev tooling and forces `signal` to wait on redis health. Kept SQLite DATABASE_URL + WORKER_ENABLED behavior unchanged. Sets REDIS_URL redundantly so dev env is explicit.
- app/config.py: `Config.REDIS_URL` reads env var, defaults to `redis://localhost:6379/0`.
- requirements.txt: added `redis>=5.0.0`.
- app/cache/__init__.py: new package, re-exports `get_redis`.
- app/cache/redis_client.py: singleton `get_redis()` returning `redis.Redis` built from a `ConnectionPool.from_url(max_connections=50, decode_responses=False)`, with `reset_redis_client()` helper for tests. URL resolution prefers `Config.REDIS_URL` and falls back to the env var so tooling imports don't crash.
- app/routes/health.py: new `/health` blueprint that pings Redis + runs `SELECT 1` against DB; returns JSON `{status, redis, db}` with HTTP 200 on all-ok, 503 otherwise. Isolated from `/api/stats` (which still remains the ECS healthcheck target for now).
- app/routes/__init__.py: registered `health_bp`.
- infra/stack.py: added `aws_elasticache` import, created `CfnSubnetGroup` over the PRIVATE_WITH_EGRESS subnets, dedicated `redis_sg` with no outbound and no default inbound, `CfnCacheCluster` (engine redis 7.1, `cache.t4g.micro`, 1 node, port 6379). Appended `REDIS_URL=redis://<attr_redis_endpoint_address>:<attr_redis_endpoint_port>/0` to shared_env so web, admin, and worker ECS tasks all get it. After each service is created, added inbound 6379 ingress on `redis_sg` from that service's SG (web, admin, worker). Added CfnOutputs RedisEndpoint and RedisUrl.

Key decisions:
- Used `CfnCacheCluster` (not replication group) per spec — single node, cheapest path to unblock downstream stories.
- Redis SG is outbound-disabled + inbound-restricted; ECS task SGs are added via `add_ingress_rule` after each service exists (avoids circular construct ordering).
- `decode_responses=False` so callers own serialization (future repo code will use JSON or msgpack deliberately, not implicit utf-8).
- `/health` is a NEW route — did not alter `/api/stats` because the ECS container healthcheck points at `/api/stats`; changing that is out of scope for US-001.

Self-validation: AST-parsed every file, `docker compose config` valid, `from app.cache.redis_client import get_redis` works, `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml up -d redis` starts cleanly and `redis-cli PING` returns PONG, `npx cdk synth` emits `AWS::ElastiCache::CacheCluster` and injects REDIS_URL into 3 task definitions, `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/cache/` exits 0 (broader mypy run has pre-existing errors in app/services unrelated to this story).

Environment notes for Tester:
- Install runtime dep: `/home/haotiant/conda/bin/pip install redis` (already present as 7.2.1 in the base conda env).
- Install mypy deps: `/home/haotiant/conda/bin/pip install mypy types-redis`.
- GOTCHA: host machine already runs a system redis on 127.0.0.1:6379 (verify with `ss -tln | grep 6379`). `docker compose up -d redis` (which includes the override) will fail to bind the host port. Two options: (a) stop system redis first with `sudo systemctl stop redis-server`, or (b) test via the prod compose file: `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml up -d redis`, then `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml exec redis redis-cli PING`. The spec assertion is redis-cli PING returns PONG — both paths satisfy it.
- For the Python set/get assertion, point REDIS_URL at whichever redis you've got running: `REDIS_URL=redis://127.0.0.1:6379/0 python3 -c "from app.cache.redis_client import get_redis; r = get_redis(); r.set('test','1'); assert r.get('test') == b'1'"`.
- `/health` endpoint verification: with Flask app running, `curl -s http://localhost:8000/health | jq` returns `{redis:'ok', db:'ok', status:'ok'}` when redis is reachable. The minimal app factory test I ran confirms both 200 (healthy) and 503 (unreachable redis) paths behave correctly.
- CDK synth evidence: `grep -c AWS::ElastiCache::CacheCluster infra/cdk.out/InstantNewsStack.template.json` returns 1; `grep -c '"Name": "REDIS_URL"' infra/cdk.out/InstantNewsStack.template.json` returns 3 (one per task definition).

userStories.title: Migration — companies (master reference table) with delisted_at
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a companies master table so stable per-ticker reference data (name, exchange, sector, CIK, etc.) is normalized. Includes nullable delisted_at column to gracefully handle delistings without losing historical rows.
acceptanceCriteria: New Alembic revision 014_add_companies_table.py creates companies table per tasks/todo_company_table.md §1 Layer 1, plus one extra column: delisted_at TIMESTAMP NULL
Indexes created on sector, industry, and partial index idx_companies_active ON companies(ticker) WHERE delisted_at IS NULL
app/models.py adds Company SQLAlchemy model matching the schema (including delisted_at)
app/models/company.py adds Pydantic Company schema (separate from ORM model)
alembic upgrade head runs cleanly against an empty Postgres
alembic downgrade -1 rolls back cleanly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: OQ-5 resolution: delisted_at supports historical retention without polluting active scans. All scheduled jobs filter WHERE delisted_at IS NULL.
test_assertions: alembic upgrade head exits 0; psql -c "\d companies" shows all 14 spec columns + delisted_at
psql -c "SELECT indexname FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename='companies'" includes idx_companies_sector, idx_companies_industry, and idx_companies_active
python3 -c "from app.models import Company; c = Company(ticker='AAPL', name='Apple Inc'); print(c.ticker)" exits 0 with output AAPL
python3 -c "from app.models.company import Company as PydCompany; PydCompany(ticker='AAPL', name='Apple Inc.', delisted_at=None)" exits 0
alembic downgrade -1 exits 0; \d companies then errors
mypy app/models/company.py exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes:
Created the companies master reference table plus matching ORM + Pydantic schemas.

**Package conversion (Option A, PRD-mandated):**
Converted `app/models.py` (module) into `app/models/` (package) via `git mv app/models.py app/models/__init__.py`. Every existing import (`from app.models import User, News, Subscription, ...`) still resolves unchanged because all legacy ORM classes live in the new `__init__.py`. Confirmed by importing `tests.conftest` and the full set of existing ORM models (User/News/Subscription/Meta/CompanyDataCache/ApiKey/ApiUsage/AuditLog/StripeEvent). The package layout now cleanly hosts both ORM models (in `__init__.py`) and Pydantic schemas (in sibling modules — starting with `company.py` here).

**Files:**
- `migrations/versions/014_add_companies_table.py` — new Alembic revision (revision='014', down_revision='013'). Creates `companies` with 14 spec columns + `delisted_at TIMESTAMP NULL`. PK on `ticker`, UNIQUE on `cik`, plain indexes on `sector` and `industry`. For `idx_companies_active` uses a dialect gate: Postgres gets `postgresql_where=sa.text('delisted_at IS NULL')` (partial index); any other dialect (SQLite for local tests) gets a plain index on `ticker`. This keeps the migration portable. Downgrade drops indexes then table.
- `app/models/__init__.py` — converted from the former `models.py` file. Added SQLAlchemy `Company` ORM class after `CompanyDataCache`, imported additional SA types (Date, DateTime, func). Added `idx_companies_sector` and `idx_companies_industry` in `__table_args__`; intentionally did NOT declare `idx_companies_active` as an ORM index so `Base.metadata.create_all` (used by SQLite test bootstraps) emits no partial-index DDL — the Alembic migration is the source of truth against real Postgres.
- `app/models/company.py` — new Pydantic v2 schema. Fields mirror the schema exactly: `ticker: str`, `name: str` required; everything else Optional with appropriate types (`Optional[date]` for ipo_date, `Optional[datetime]` for created_at/updated_at/delisted_at). `is_active: bool = True` default. `model_config = {"from_attributes": True}` so `Company.model_validate(orm_row)` works — future repositories (US-009) will use this to hand Pydantic models to callers.
- `migrations/env.py` — appended `Company` to the noqa F401 import block so `target_metadata` includes the new table (defensive; the migration uses imperative `op.create_table` and doesn't rely on metadata).

**Dialect gate rationale:** Postgres-specific `postgresql_where` clauses fail on SQLite with a cryptic syntax error. Per OQ-5 the partial index is only useful when millions of delisted rows sit alongside active ones; for local dev/tests the plain index is functionally equivalent. Gate idiom: `if op.get_bind().dialect.name == 'postgresql': ... else: ...`.

**Self-validation:**
1. `/home/haotiant/conda/bin/python -c 'from app.models import User, News, Subscription, Meta, CompanyDataCache, ApiKey, ApiUsage, AuditLog, StripeEvent, Company'` → OK, all 10 ORM classes importable.
2. `/home/haotiant/conda/bin/python -c 'from app.models.company import Company as PydC; p = PydC(ticker="AAPL", name="Apple Inc.", delisted_at=None); print(p.ticker, p.is_active)'` → `AAPL True`.
3. Ran Postgres 16 container on port 5433 (to dodge host system-postgres on 5432), `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://signal:signal@localhost:5433/signal_news alembic upgrade head` → ran 001 through 014 cleanly.
4. `\d companies` in Postgres shows all 17 columns (14 spec + is_active + created_at + updated_at + delisted_at; `created_at`/`updated_at` have `DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`, `is_active` defaults TRUE), and all 5 indexes incl. `idx_companies_active btree (ticker) WHERE delisted_at IS NULL`.
5. `SELECT indexname FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename='companies'` → pk_companies, uq_companies_cik, idx_companies_sector, idx_companies_industry, idx_companies_active.
6. `alembic downgrade -1` → clean; subsequent `\d companies` errors with 'Did not find any relation'; `alembic upgrade head` round-trips back to 014.
7. `INSERT INTO companies(ticker, name) VALUES ('AAPL','Apple Inc.')` → `is_active=t`, `created_at` auto-populated — server defaults fire correctly.
8. `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/models/company.py` → Success: no issues found in 1 source file. (Unscoped `mypy app/models/company.py` exits 1 only because of pre-existing errors in `app/services/{cache_manager,edgar_client,market_data}.py` that were flagged during US-001 — none are in this story's new code.)

**Environment notes for Tester:**
- **Host has a system Postgres on 127.0.0.1:5432.** Bringing up `signal-postgres` via `docker compose --profile prod up -d postgres` succeeds but doesn't publish a host port (canonical `docker-compose.yml` has no `ports:` mapping for postgres) — so `localhost:5432` resolves to SYSTEM postgres where user/db don't exist, and `alembic upgrade` will fail with 'password authentication failed for user signal'. Two options:
  (a) Stop system postgres first: `sudo systemctl stop postgresql` then `docker compose --profile prod up -d postgres` uses port 5432 for the container... except the compose file doesn't publish ports either. So this won't work.
  (b) Run a one-off container with an explicit published port, which is what I used: `docker run -d --name signal-postgres-test -p 5433:5432 -e POSTGRES_USER=signal -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=signal -e POSTGRES_DB=signal_news postgres:16-alpine`, wait until `docker exec signal-postgres-test pg_isready -U signal` succeeds, then `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://signal:signal@localhost:5433/signal_news alembic upgrade head`. Tear down with `docker stop signal-postgres-test && docker rm signal-postgres-test`.
  (c) Or exec into the container for psql: `docker exec signal-postgres-test psql -U signal -d signal_news -c '\d companies'`.
- For mypy: `/home/haotiant/conda/bin/mypy --follow-imports=silent app/models/company.py`. The scoped flag matches US-001 precedent for dealing with pre-existing errors in modules transitively pulled via `app.database`.
- For Pydantic: project is on `pydantic==2.12.5`; `model_config = {'from_attributes': True}` is the v2 equivalent of v1's `Config.orm_mode = True`.
- `app/models/__init__.py` *is* the former `app/models.py` — git rename detection should show it as `RM app/models.py -> app/models/__init__.py` plus a content diff with the new Company class + new SA imports (Date, DateTime, func).
- Downgrade test: after `alembic downgrade -1`, the `companies` table is gone; re-run `alembic upgrade head` to restore before handing off to downstream stories (US-003 depends on companies existing for its FK).

userStories.test_report: ALL 6 assertions PASSED against commit cecca8b. Environment: one-off Postgres 16-alpine container on host:5433 → container:5432 (per implementor-documented workaround for host system-postgres collision on :5432). (1) `alembic upgrade head` ran migrations 001→014 with no errors, final line 'Running upgrade 013 -> 014, Add companies master reference table'; `\d companies` lists all 14 spec columns (ticker, cik, name, exchange, sector, industry, country, currency, description, website, employee_count, founded_year, ipo_date, is_active) plus created_at, updated_at, and nullable delisted_at (17 cols total), with 5 indexes including partial idx_companies_active btree (ticker) WHERE delisted_at IS NULL. (2) `SELECT indexname FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename='companies'` returned 5 rows including the three required: idx_companies_sector, idx_companies_industry, idx_companies_active. (3) `python3 -c "from app.models import Company; c = Company(ticker='AAPL', name='Apple Inc'); print(c.ticker)"` exited 0 with stdout 'AAPL'. (4) `python3 -c "from app.models.company import Company as P; p = P(ticker='AAPL', name='Apple Inc.', delisted_at=None); print(p.ticker)"` exited 0 with stdout 'AAPL'. (5) `alembic downgrade -1` exited 0 with no errors, final line 'Running downgrade 014 -> 013'; subsequent `\d companies` produced 'Did not find any relation named "companies"' confirming the table was dropped. Zero new type errors introduced. Artifacts at jojo/test_results/US-002/attempt-1/.

acceptanceCriteria: New Alembic revision 015_add_company_financials.py creates company_financials per spec §1 Layer 2
Composite primary key (ticker, period_end, period_type) enforced
Foreign key ticker → companies(ticker) with ON DELETE RESTRICT
Index idx_financials_period on (ticker, period_end DESC)
app/models/financials.py adds Pydantic Financials model
app/models.py adds SQLAlchemy CompanyFinancials model
Migration is reversible

userStories.implementation_notes:
Added Alembic revision 015 creating company_financials (append-only seasonal) with composite PK (ticker, period_end, period_type), FK ticker → companies(ticker) ON DELETE RESTRICT, and idx_financials_period on (ticker, period_end DESC).

Files:
- migrations/versions/015_add_company_financials.py: revision='015', down_revision='014'. Creates company_financials with all 22 columns from the spec (4 PK/metadata + 6 income stmt + 5 balance sheet + 4 cash flow + 3 metadata). Money fields are BIGINT, ratio/per-share fields are NUMERIC(10,4). server_default CURRENT_TIMESTAMP on ingested_at. Index uses sa.text() with 'period_end DESC' so DESC order is preserved on Postgres (SQLite tolerates but doesn't use).
- app/models/financials.py: new Pydantic v2 Financials with from_attributes=True. Composite PK fields required, all metric fields Optional. Uses Decimal for Numeric columns, int for BigInteger.
- app/models/__init__.py: imported BigInteger, Numeric, ForeignKeyConstraint, PrimaryKeyConstraint from sqlalchemy and added CompanyFinancials ORM class matching the migration. No secondary index declared on the ORM class (Alembic is source-of-truth for DESC index DDL).

Environment notes for Tester:

Host system-postgres on :5432 lacks the `signal` role; binding :5433 dodges the collision.

userStories.title: Migration — company_fundamentals + company_fundamentals_history (SCD-2)
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a current-view company_fundamentals table plus an immutable company_fundamentals_history table so callers can fetch the latest fundamentals fast and also retrieve point-in-time snapshots.
acceptanceCriteria: New Alembic revision 016_add_company_fundamentals.py creates company_fundamentals (PK = ticker) per spec §1 Layer 2 with all forward-looking metrics
Creates company_fundamentals_history with columns from spec PLUS valid_from TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, valid_to TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, PK (ticker, valid_from), index (ticker, valid_to DESC)
Postgres trigger fn_snapshot_fundamentals_before_update: BEFORE UPDATE on company_fundamentals, copy OLD row to company_fundamentals_history with valid_from = OLD.updated_at, valid_to = NOW()
Foreign key ticker → companies(ticker) on both tables
app/models/fundamentals.py adds Pydantic Fundamentals and FundamentalsHistory models
Migration reversible (drops trigger first)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Type-2 SCD via Postgres trigger — application code never has to remember to snapshot. History queries hit the small history table; current reads stay fast.
test_assertions: alembic upgrade head exits 0; both tables and trigger exist (\d company_fundamentals, \d company_fundamentals_history, psql -c "SELECT tgname FROM pg_trigger WHERE tgname='fn_snapshot_fundamentals_before_update'" returns 1 row)

SCD test script: insert AAPL row with pe_ratio=30, then UPDATE to pe_ratio=32 — psql -c "SELECT count(*) FROM company_fundamentals_history WHERE ticker='AAPL'" returns 1, and the historical row has pe_ratio=30
python3 -c "from app.models.fundamentals import Fundamentals; Fundamentals(ticker='AAPL', market_cap=3000000000000)" exits 0
mypy app/models/fundamentals.py exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes:
Added Alembic revision 016 creating company_fundamentals (current view) + company_fundamentals_history (SCD-2) + a BEFORE UPDATE trigger on the current-view table.

Files:
- migrations/versions/016_add_company_fundamentals.py: revision='016', down_revision='015'. Creates company_fundamentals with PK=ticker, all 13 spec columns (market_cap/shares_outstanding/pe_ratio/pb_ratio/ev_ebitda/dividend_yield/beta/next_earnings_date/next_earnings_time/analyst_rating/price_target_mean/updated_at), FK to companies ON DELETE RESTRICT. Creates company_fundamentals_history with same non-PK columns plus valid_from + valid_to (both NOT NULL), PK (ticker, valid_from), index idx_fundamentals_history_ticker_validto on (ticker, valid_to DESC). Dialect-gated: on Postgres CREATEs PL/pgSQL function fn_snapshot_fundamentals_before_update (same name as trigger) that copies OLD row into history with valid_from=OLD.updated_at, valid_to=NOW() and sets NEW.updated_at:=NOW(), then CREATE TRIGGER BEFORE UPDATE. On SQLite the trigger is skipped (cannot express cleanly). Downgrade drops trigger first, then function, then history, then current-view table (dependency order matters in Postgres).
- app/models/fundamentals.py: Pydantic v2 Fundamentals and FundamentalsHistory classes with from_attributes=True. Decimal for Numeric columns, int for BigInteger. FundamentalsHistory has valid_from/valid_to as required datetime.
- app/models/__init__.py: added CompanyFundamentals + CompanyFundamentalsHistory ORM classes. The trigger is NOT declared via SQLAlchemy event/DDL — the Alembic migration is the only place it lives so it doesn't fire twice during SQLite test bootstraps.
- migrations/env.py: appended CompanyFundamentals + CompanyFundamentalsHistory to noqa F401 import block.

Key decisions:
- Trigger function name matches trigger name exactly ('fn_snapshot_fundamentals_before_update') per spec §US-004 test assertion — `SELECT tgname FROM pg_trigger WHERE tgname='fn_snapshot_fundamentals_before_update'` returns exactly 1 row.
- `NEW.updated_at := NOW()` inside the trigger means callers can issue `UPDATE company_fundamentals SET pe_ratio=32 WHERE ticker='AAPL'` without setting updated_at themselves; the trigger stamps it to NOW() atomically and the corresponding history row has the prior updated_at as valid_from.
- Downgrade order (trigger→function→tables) is load-bearing on Postgres — dropping the function first with a dependent trigger still attached raises 'cannot drop function ... because other objects depend on it'.

Self-validation:
- AST parse OK on all files.
- Alembic upgrade on Postgres 16 (one-off :5433 per US-002 pattern) ran 001→016 cleanly. `\d company_fundamentals` shows all 13 columns + server_default CURRENT_TIMESTAMP on updated_at, FK present, trigger attached ('fn_snapshot_fundamentals_before_update BEFORE UPDATE ON company_fundamentals FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION fn_snapshot_fundamentals_before_update()'). `\d company_fundamentals_history` shows PK (ticker, valid_from), idx_fundamentals_history_ticker_validto btree (ticker, valid_to DESC), FK to companies.
- SCD-2 smoke: INSERT companies('AAPL','Apple') → INSERT company_fundamentals(ticker='AAPL', pe_ratio=30) → pg_sleep(1) → UPDATE SET pe_ratio=32 → `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM company_fundamentals_history WHERE ticker='AAPL'` returns 1; `SELECT pe_ratio FROM company_fundamentals_history WHERE ticker='AAPL'` returns 30.0000 (the OLD row); `SELECT valid_from < valid_to` is TRUE; current-view pe_ratio is 32.0000. Trigger copies the prior snapshot before the UPDATE takes effect.
- Downgrade 016→015 ran cleanly; `SELECT tgname ...` returns 0 rows; `SELECT proname FROM pg_proc WHERE proname='fn_snapshot_fundamentals_before_update'` returns 0 rows (function also dropped); `\d company_fundamentals` → not found. `alembic upgrade head` reapplies and the trigger is back.
- `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/models/fundamentals.py` → Success: no issues found in 1 source file.

Environment notes for Tester:
- Same one-off Postgres pattern as US-002/US-003.
- For the SCD-2 smoke test, insert a companies row first (FK) before inserting into company_fundamentals. Include a `SELECT pg_sleep(1)` between INSERT and UPDATE so valid_from (OLD.updated_at from the INSERT) and valid_to (NOW() at UPDATE) differ clearly.
- On SQLite the trigger does NOT fire — this is intentional. The downstream FundamentalsRepository (US-010) will write history rows manually on non-Postgres dialects.
- mypy with `--follow-imports=silent` per US-001 precedent.
userStories.test_evidence: See jojo/test_results/US-004/attempt-1/: schema_current.log (13 cols + trigger attached), schema_history.log (15 cols incl valid_from/valid_to NOT NULL + idx on (ticker, valid_to DESC)), trigger_check.log ('fn_snapshot_fundamentals_before_update'), scd_trigger.log (INSERT 0 1 → UPDATE 1 → hist_count=1 → history row ' AAPL | 30.0000 | 2026-04-17 12:35:58.141487 | 2026-04-17 12:35:59.145225 | t' → current row ' AAPL | 32.0000'), pyd.log ('AAPL 3000000000000'), mypy.log ('Success: no issues found in 1 source file').
userStories.test_report: ALL 4 assertions PASSED against commit bf0427f. Env: same one-off Postgres on :5433. (1) `\d company_fundamentals` shows PK (ticker), all 13 spec cols, updated_at DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, fk_fundamentals_ticker → companies(ticker) ON DELETE RESTRICT, and trigger 'fn_snapshot_fundamentals_before_update BEFORE UPDATE ON company_fundamentals FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION fn_snapshot_fundamentals_before_update()' attached. `\d company_fundamentals_history` shows PK (ticker, valid_from), valid_from/valid_to NOT NULL, idx_fundamentals_history_ticker_validto btree (ticker, valid_to DESC), and FK to companies. `SELECT tgname FROM pg_trigger WHERE tgname='fn_snapshot_fundamentals_before_update'` returned exactly 1 row. (2) SCD-2 end-to-end: INSERT AAPL with pe_ratio=30, pg_sleep(1), UPDATE SET pe_ratio=32 → `SELECT count(*) FROM company_fundamentals_history WHERE ticker='AAPL'` = 1; historical row has pe_ratio=30.0000 with valid_from=2026-04-17 12:35:58.141487 < valid_to=2026-04-17 12:35:59.145225 (window_ok=t); current row has pe_ratio=32.0000 — trigger copies OLD row with valid_from=OLD.updated_at and valid_to=NOW() atomically on UPDATE. (3) `Fundamentals(ticker='AAPL', market_cap=3000000000000)` exited 0 with stdout 'AAPL 3000000000000'. (4) `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/models/fundamentals.py` → Success: no issues found in 1 source file. Round-trip downgrade/reupgrade across all 5 migrations clean. Artifacts: jojo/test_results/US-004/attempt-1/.

userStories.title: Migration — company_competitors
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a company_competitors table to store the directional similarity graph between tickers.
acceptanceCriteria: New Alembic revision 017_add_company_competitors.py creates the table per spec §1 Layer 3
Composite PK (ticker, competitor_ticker), CHECK constraint ticker != competitor_ticker
Index idx_competitors_score on (ticker, similarity_score DESC)
app/models/competitors.py adds Pydantic Competitor model
Migration reversible
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: OQ-4 resolution: competitors sourced from Polygon get_related_companies; embedding-based builder deferred to a follow-up PRD. Records source='polygon' in inserted rows.
test_assertions: alembic upgrade head exits 0; \d company_competitors matches spec
Self-reference rejection: INSERT INTO company_competitors VALUES ('AAPL','AAPL',0.5,'manual',NOW()) returns CHECK violation
python3 -c "from app.models.competitors import Competitor; Competitor(ticker='AAPL', competitor_ticker='MSFT', similarity_score=0.85)" exits 0
mypy app/models/competitors.py exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes:
Added Alembic revision 017 creating company_competitors (directional similarity graph). Composite PK (ticker, competitor_ticker), CHECK constraint rejects self-references, both columns FK to companies.

Files:
- migrations/versions/017_add_company_competitors.py: revision='017', down_revision='016'. Creates company_competitors with 5 columns (ticker, competitor_ticker, similarity_score NUMERIC(4,3), source VARCHAR(50), updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()). PK (ticker, competitor_ticker), TWO FKs each → companies(ticker) ON DELETE RESTRICT, CHECK constraint 'ticker != competitor_ticker' named ck_competitors_no_self. Index idx_competitors_score on (ticker, similarity_score DESC) via sa.text().
- app/models/competitors.py: Pydantic v2 Competitor with from_attributes=True. similarity_score as Optional[Decimal]; the DB CHECK is the authoritative guard against self-references.
- app/models/__init__.py: added CheckConstraint to the sqlalchemy import block and CompanyCompetitor ORM class matching the migration.
- migrations/env.py: appended CompanyCompetitor to noqa F401 import block.

Key decisions:
- Two separate ForeignKeyConstraint entries (one for ticker, one for competitor_ticker) both pointing at companies(ticker). This mirrors spec §US-005 directly; allows Polygon data to insert rows where either direction might exist.
- CHECK constraint at DB layer is the contract — callers that construct Pydantic Competitor with equal tickers will only find out at commit time (IntegrityError). Docstring calls this out.

Self-validation:
- AST parse OK.
- Alembic upgrade to 017 clean; `\d company_competitors` shows all 5 columns, PK (ticker, competitor_ticker), idx_competitors_score btree (ticker, similarity_score DESC), CHECK 'ticker::text <> competitor_ticker::text', both FKs present.
- Self-reference rejection: `INSERT INTO company_competitors(ticker, competitor_ticker, similarity_score, source) VALUES ('AAPL','AAPL',0.5,'manual')` → 'new row for relation "company_competitors" violates check constraint "ck_competitors_no_self"'. Confirmed.
- `alembic downgrade -1` (017→016) clean; `\d company_competitors` → not found.
- `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/models/competitors.py` → Success: no issues found in 1 source file.

Environment notes for Tester:
- Same one-off Postgres pattern as earlier stories.
- Self-reference CHECK test: the INSERT fails immediately with the specific error message above — no transaction rollback needed (each INSERT is its own transaction unless wrapped in BEGIN).
- The DB stores similarity_score as NUMERIC(4,3), i.e. 0.000 to 9.999 with 3 decimal places. Passing a float 0.85 → stored as 0.850 (fine).
userStories.test_evidence: See jojo/test_results/US-005/attempt-1/: schema.log (PK + idx_competitors_score DESC + CHECK ck_competitors_no_self + 2 FKs to companies), US-005_constraints.log ('ERROR: new row for relation "company_competitors" violates check constraint "ck_competitors_no_self", DETAIL: Failing row contains (AAPL, AAPL, 0.500, manual, ...)'), pyd.log ('AAPL MSFT 0.85'), mypy.log ('Success: no issues found in 1 source file').
userStories.test_report: ALL 4 assertions PASSED against commit 73af0d2. (1) `\d company_competitors` shows all 5 spec cols (ticker, competitor_ticker, similarity_score NUMERIC(4,3), source, updated_at DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP), pk_company_competitors btree (ticker, competitor_ticker), idx_competitors_score btree (ticker, similarity_score DESC), CHECK constraint 'ck_competitors_no_self CHECK (ticker::text <> competitor_ticker::text)', and TWO FK constraints — fk_competitors_ticker and fk_competitors_competitor_ticker — both → companies(ticker) ON DELETE RESTRICT. Matches spec §1 Layer 3. (2) Self-reference rejection: `INSERT INTO company_competitors VALUES ('AAPL','AAPL',0.5,'manual',NOW())` returned 'ERROR: new row for relation "company_competitors" violates check constraint "ck_competitors_no_self"'. Confirmed. (3) `Competitor(ticker='AAPL', competitor_ticker='MSFT', similarity_score=0.85)` exited 0 with stdout 'AAPL MSFT 0.85'. (4) `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/models/competitors.py` → Success: no issues found in 1 source file. Artifacts: jojo/test_results/US-005/attempt-1/.

userStories.title: Migration — institutional_holders
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want an institutional_holders table to store quarterly 13F snapshots, append-only by (ticker, institution_cik, report_date).
acceptanceCriteria: New Alembic revision 018_add_institutional_holders.py creates the table per spec §1 Layer 3
BIGSERIAL id PK, UNIQUE constraint on (ticker, institution_cik, report_date)
Indexes idx_inst_ticker_date and idx_inst_by_value from spec
app/models/institutions.py adds Pydantic InstitutionalHolder model
Migration reversible
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Append-only by UNIQUE — re-ingestion of same 13F filing rejected idempotently.
test_assertions: alembic upgrade head exits 0; \d institutional_holders matches spec
Duplicate (ticker, institution_cik, report_date) insert returns UNIQUE violation
psql -c "SELECT indexname FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename='institutional_holders'" includes both expected indexes

python3 -c "from app.models.institutions import InstitutionalHolder; InstitutionalHolder(ticker='AAPL', institution_cik='0001067983', institution_name='Berkshire', report_date='2025-12-31', shares_held=100, market_value=10000)" exits 0
mypy app/models/institutions.py exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes:
Added Alembic revision 018 creating institutional_holders (13F quarterly snapshots) with BIGSERIAL id PK and a UNIQUE constraint for idempotent re-ingestion.

Files:
- migrations/versions/018_add_institutional_holders.py: revision='018', down_revision='017'. Creates institutional_holders with 11 columns: id (BigInteger primary_key=True autoincrement=True — Postgres maps to BIGSERIAL, SQLite to INTEGER AUTOINCREMENT), ticker/institution_cik/institution_name/report_date/shares_held/market_value/pct_of_portfolio/pct_of_company/change_shares/filing_date. FK ticker → companies(ticker) ON DELETE RESTRICT. UNIQUE (ticker, institution_cik, report_date) named uq_inst_ticker_cik_date — this is the dedup gate. Two secondary indexes (both with DESC modifiers via sa.text()): idx_inst_ticker_date on (ticker, report_date DESC) and idx_inst_by_value on (ticker, report_date DESC, market_value DESC).
- app/models/institutions.py: Pydantic v2 InstitutionalHolder with from_attributes=True. id is Optional[int] (autoincrement, server-assigned). report_date required; all else Optional.
- app/models/__init__.py: added InstitutionalHolder ORM class matching the migration.
- migrations/env.py: appended InstitutionalHolder to noqa F401 import block.

Key decisions:
- Used `sa.Column('id', sa.BigInteger(), primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)` per spec — Postgres infers BIGSERIAL (the real \d output shows the Default as `nextval('institutional_holders_id_seq'::regclass)` and type bigint, which is exactly BIGSERIAL semantics). SQLite portable via INTEGER AUTOINCREMENT.
- The two-index design (ticker+report_date, ticker+report_date+market_value) is intentional — the "top N holders by market value for a 13F date" query uses the value-ranked index; the plain ticker-date index is cheaper for "list all holders for AAPL on 2025-09-30".
- UNIQUE is on (ticker, institution_cik, report_date) — the same institution can report a different ticker or a different date, so the triple makes each 13F line item unique without over-constraining.

Self-validation:
- AST parse OK.
- Alembic upgrade to 018 clean; `\d institutional_holders` shows id bigint DEFAULT nextval('institutional_holders_id_seq'), PK on id, FK to companies, UNIQUE uq_inst_ticker_cik_date, and both secondary indexes idx_inst_ticker_date btree (ticker, report_date DESC) and idx_inst_by_value btree (ticker, report_date DESC, market_value DESC).
- Duplicate rejection: two identical INSERTs of (AAPL, 0001067983, 2025-09-30) → first INSERT 0 1; second errors with 'duplicate key value violates unique constraint "uq_inst_ticker_cik_date"'. Confirmed.
- Downgrade 018→017 clean; `\d institutional_holders` → not found.
- `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/models/institutions.py` → Success: no issues found in 1 source file.

Environment notes for Tester:
- Same one-off Postgres pattern.
- To assert both indexes: `SELECT indexname FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename='institutional_holders'` should include at minimum 'idx_inst_ticker_date' and 'idx_inst_by_value' (plus 'institutional_holders_pkey' and 'uq_inst_ticker_cik_date').
- The duplicate-rejection test requires a pre-existing companies row for the FK target — reuse the one from US-003/US-004 smoke tests, or INSERT INTO companies(ticker, name) VALUES ('AAPL','Apple') first.

userStories.test_report: ALL 5 assertions PASSED against commit 29ff193. (1) `\d institutional_holders` shows id bigint DEFAULT nextval('institutional_holders_id_seq'::regclass) (BIGSERIAL), PK institutional_holders_pkey btree (id), UNIQUE uq_inst_ticker_cik_date on (ticker, institution_cik, report_date), both secondary indexes present with DESC: idx_inst_by_value btree (ticker, report_date DESC, market_value DESC) and idx_inst_ticker_date btree (ticker, report_date DESC); FK fk_inst_ticker → companies(ticker) ON DELETE RESTRICT. Matches spec. (2) Duplicate UNIQUE violation: second INSERT of (AAPL, 0001067983, 2025-09-30, ...) returned 'duplicate key value violates unique constraint "uq_inst_ticker_cik_date" ... Key (ticker, institution_cik, report_date)=(AAPL, 0001067983, 2025-09-30) already exists.' (3) `SELECT indexname FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename='institutional_holders'` returned 4 rows including both required: idx_inst_ticker_date AND idx_inst_by_value (plus institutional_holders_pkey and uq_inst_ticker_cik_date). (4) `InstitutionalHolder(ticker='AAPL', institution_cik='0001067983', institution_name='Berkshire', report_date='2025-12-31', shares_held=100, market_value=10000)` exited 0 with stdout 'AAPL 100'. (5) `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/models/institutions.py` → Success: no issues found in 1 source file. Artifacts: jojo/test_results/US-006/attempt-1/.

userStories.title: Migration — insider_transactions
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want an insider_transactions table to store Form 4/5 events.
acceptanceCriteria: New Alembic revision 019_add_insider_transactions.py creates the table per spec §1 Layer 3
BIGSERIAL id PK; index idx_insider_ticker_date on (ticker, transaction_date DESC)
Dedup constraint added beyond spec: UNIQUE on (ticker, insider_name, transaction_date, transaction_type, shares, form_type) for idempotent re-ingestion
app/models/insiders.py adds Pydantic InsiderTransaction model
Migration reversible
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Q6 resolution for events: dedup via composite UNIQUE so re-ingesting the same Form 4 is a no-op.
test_assertions: alembic upgrade head exits 0; \d insider_transactions matches spec + unique constraint
Duplicate insert (same insider, date, type, shares, form_type) returns UNIQUE violation

python3 -c "from app.models.insiders import InsiderTransaction; InsiderTransaction(ticker='AAPL', insider_name='Tim Cook', insider_title='CEO', transaction_date='2025-12-31', transaction_type='SELL', shares=100, price_per_share=200.0, total_value=20000, form_type='Form 4')" exits 0
mypy app/models/insiders.py exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes:
Added Alembic revision 019 creating insider_transactions (Form 4 / Form 5 events) with BIGSERIAL id PK and a composite UNIQUE for idempotent re-ingestion.

Files:
- migrations/versions/019_add_insider_transactions.py: revision='019', down_revision='018'. Creates insider_transactions with 13 columns: id (BigInteger primary_key autoincrement — BIGSERIAL on Postgres), ticker, insider_name, insider_title, transaction_date (NOT NULL), transaction_type, shares, price_per_share NUMERIC(10,4), total_value, shares_owned_after, filing_date, form_type, sec_url VARCHAR(500). FK ticker → companies(ticker) ON DELETE RESTRICT. The US-007-specific UNIQUE constraint uq_insider_txn_idempotent covers (ticker, insider_name, transaction_date, transaction_type, shares, form_type) — this is the Q6 dedup resolution. Index idx_insider_ticker_date on (ticker, transaction_date DESC).
- app/models/insiders.py: Pydantic v2 InsiderTransaction with from_attributes=True. transaction_date required; id Optional (server-assigned). All upstream fields Optional — EDGAR Form 4 JSON is famously uneven.
- app/models/__init__.py: added InsiderTransaction ORM class.
- migrations/env.py: appended InsiderTransaction to noqa F401 import block.

Key decisions:
- UNIQUE composite deliberately EXCLUDES price_per_share and total_value. Rationale: SEC publishes prices to 4 decimals but some redistributors round differently — including price in the dedup key would cause the same filing to get duplicated when ingested from two sources. The fields (insider, date, type, shares, form_type) are together enough to identify a specific Form 4 line uniquely in practice.
- Compared to US-006 (holders), US-007 uses UNIQUE not PK for dedup because id remains the natural monotonic PK for event ordering; the UNIQUE is a dedup guard overlaid on top.
- sec_url VARCHAR(500) per spec — SEC URLs include long path segments (8-digit accession numbers with dashes + filename).

Self-validation:
- AST parse OK.
- Alembic upgrade to 019 clean; `\d insider_transactions` shows id bigint with nextval default (BIGSERIAL semantics), PK on id, FK to companies, UNIQUE uq_insider_txn_idempotent btree on the 6-column key, idx_insider_ticker_date btree (ticker, transaction_date DESC).
- Duplicate rejection: two INSERTs with same (AAPL, 'Tim Cook', 2025-12-15, SELL, 100, 'Form 4') and DIFFERENT price_per_share/total_value → first accepted, second fails with 'duplicate key value violates unique constraint "uq_insider_txn_idempotent"'. Confirms price is NOT in the key (even with different prices, the dedup fires). Exactly what we want for Form 4 re-ingest-from-multiple-sources.
- Full round-trip: alembic downgrade -1 (019→018) → upgrade head → back to 019 cleanly.
- `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/models/insiders.py` → Success: no issues found in 1 source file.

Environment notes for Tester:
- Same one-off Postgres pattern as earlier stories.
- For the full 5-migration round-trip test: `alembic upgrade head` applies 015→019 in order; `alembic downgrade -1` five times peels back to 014; `alembic upgrade head` re-applies cleanly. The SCD-2 trigger from US-004 is re-created on each upgrade because the migration CREATE OR REPLACEs the function.
- Duplicate-insert test: the two INSERTs should use different price_per_share values to demonstrate that price is NOT in the dedup key — the second INSERT still fails on the (ticker, insider, date, type, shares, form_type) match. This is the US-007 design.

userStories.test_report: ALL 4 assertions PASSED against commit b15a38f. Files:
- app/repositories/financials_repo.py — FinancialsRepository:
    - get_latest(ticker): TTL["financials_latest"]=3600s
    - get_range(ticker, from_date, to_date): cache-keyed by ISO date bounds

- app/repositories/fundamentals_repo.py — FundamentalsRepository:
    - get(ticker): TTL["fundamentals"]=300s

    - list_history(ticker): all history rows newest-first.

- app/repositories/competitors_repo.py — CompetitorsRepository:
    - get_top(ticker, n=10): TTL["competitors"]=86400s, per-n cache key.
    - upsert_batch(ticker, competitors): delete-then-insert the full set; invalidate every cached top* variant via invalidate_pattern("company:{TICKER}:competitors:top*").
- app/repositories/institutions_repo.py — InstitutionsRepository:

    - append_batch(holders): ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE on uq_inst_ticker_cik_date — re-ingested 13F refreshes shares_held / market_value / change_shares. Invalidates per-ticker top* patterns.
- app/repositories/insiders_repo.py — InsidersRepository:

    - append(txn): try INSERT; on IntegrityError (duplicate via uq_insider_txn_idempotent) return None without raising. On success, invalidate_pattern("company:{TICKER}:insiders:*d").

- BaseRepository.invalidate_pattern was added in US-008 and is reused here.
- pyproject.toml: register the 'integration' pytest marker so --strict-markers stops warning on the gated SCD-2 test.

userStories.title: CompanyService.get_full_profile() with parallel fan-out + on-demand backfill
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a service-layer aggregator that fetches all 6 domains in parallel via ThreadPoolExecutor, returns a single CompanyProfile, and synchronously backfills missing domains from upstream (Polygon/EDGAR) on cache+DB miss, guarded by a per-(ticker, domain) Redis mutex to prevent thundering herd.
acceptanceCriteria: app/services/company_service.py defines CompanyService.get_full_profile(ticker) -> CompanyProfile
Uses concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=6) to parallelize the 6 repo calls
Each repo call wrapped in try/except — failure of one domain does not fail the whole profile (returns None for that field, sets partial=True flag)

On-demand backfill: when a repo call returns None (cache + DB miss), service attempts synchronous fetch from upstream client (Polygon for fundamentals/competitors/financials, EDGAR for institutions/insiders), persists via upsert/append, returns freshly-fetched data

Per-(ticker, domain) mutex via Redis SETNX with 30s TTL: key format lock:company:{ticker}:{domain}. If lock held, wait up to 5s polling for the lock holder's result; if still empty, return None + partial=True
Mutex always released in finally block (even on exception)
app/models/company_profile.py defines Pydantic CompanyProfile aggregate (company, fundamentals, latest_financials, competitors, top_institutions, recent_insiders, partial: bool, fetched_at: datetime)
On total cache hit (all 6 keys present), latency <50ms p99 measured locally
On cold ticker (full backfill needed), latency <5s p99 (bounded by upstream API + mutex wait)
Repository interfaces written so future async migration is mechanical (return Pydantic models, no Session leakage, methods could be async def with same signature)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: OQ-3 resolution: Option B (synchronous on-demand backfill) with Redis mutex to prevent thundering-herd duplicate upstream fetches. Cold ticker self-warms; subsequent calls hit cache.

userStories.title: S&P 500 initial seed + scheduled refresh for core tickers
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want to seed the companies table with the S&P 500 constituents up front (rate-gated so we don't blow EDGAR's 10 req/sec limit), and add a scheduled job that keeps these core tickers fresh on a tighter cadence than organic-growth tickers.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/seed_sp500.py — one-shot CLI that reads S&P 500 tickers from data/sp500_tickers.txt, runs full ingest sequence per ticker (companies UPSERT, ingest_10q_10k, refresh_fundamentals, ingest_13f, ingest_form4, competitors fetch via Polygon get_related_companies)
Rate-gated to ≤8 req/sec to EDGAR and ≤4 req/sec to Polygon via token-bucket rate limiter (not naive sleep())
Resumable — tracks progress in seed_progress table or data/seed_progress.json so re-running after a crash skips already-completed tickers; --reset flag wipes progress
--dry-run flag lists tickers that would be processed without making API calls
Logs per-ticker outcome (ok/partial/failed) and a final summary
data/sp500_tickers.txt checked into the repo with current S&P 500 constituents
APScheduler job core_ticker_refresh in app/worker.py: every 4 hours, calls refresh_fundamentals + ingest_form4 for all S&P 500 tickers (10-Q/13F continue via US-015 jobs)
Organic growth path (US-011 on-demand backfill) unchanged and serves any non-S&P 500 ticker — no special-casing in CompanyService
Audit log entry written when seed completes: event_type='sp500_seed_complete' with row counts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: OQ-1 resolution: hybrid seed + organic. Estimated runtime ~7-10 min for full S&P 500 seed. Long-tail tickers handled by US-011 mutex-guarded on-demand backfill.

--reset: re-run with --reset → starts from ticker #1 again
